title: Hindi Alphabets – Devanagari Alphabets
url: Hindi Alphabets – Devanagari Alphabets
content: Hindi is written in Devanagari. Learn independent vowels (स्वर), consonants (व्यंजन), and example words as in standard वर्णमाला charts.
intro: Hindi uses the same Devanagari letters as many North Indian languages. Each consonant has an inherent short ‘a’ unless a matra or halant is added.

title: Vowels, Consonants in Hindi and their pronunciation
url: Vowels, Consonants in Hindi and their pronunciation
content: Hindi vowels and consonants: clear vowel length (अ vs आ), aspiration (क vs ख), and retroflex vs dental (त vs ट).
intro: Listen for the puff of air on aspirated letters (ख, घ, छ, झ, etc.) and for tongue position on ट-वर्ग vs त-वर्ग.

blocks.heading: Pronunciation notes
blocks.content: Short अ is like the ‘u’ in ‘sugar’; long आ is like ‘father’. Retroflex ट ठ ड ढ ण use the tongue curled back; dental त थ द ध न use the tip behind the teeth.

title: Hindi Barakhadi: Symbols for vowels with consonants
url: Hindi Barakhadi: Symbols for vowels with consonants
content: Barakhadi (बारहखड़ी): each consonant combines with matras — the twelve vowel sounds after क are का, कि, की, कु, कू, के, कै, को, कौ, कं, कः (patterns follow क for all letters).
intro: The inherent vowel is schwa (short a). Matras are: ा ि ी ु ू े ै ो ौ ं ः. Same sequence is learned for ख, ग, … ह in school charts.

blocks.heading: क + matras (model barakhadi row)
blocks.content: क (ka), का (kā), कि (ki), की (kī), कु (ku), कू (kū), के (ke), कै (kai), को (ko), कौ (kau), कं (kaṃ), कः (kaḥ). Replace क with any consonant for a full barakhadi chart.

title: Frequently faced pronunciation problems in Hindi (Devanagari)
url: Frequently faced pronunciation problems in Hindi (Devanagari)
content: Common mistakes: mixing dental त and retroflex ट; aspiration (क vs ख); and nasal vowels vs अनुस्वार.
intro: Use minimal pairs below — same English gloss shows the contrast you should hear.

blocks.heading: Tips
blocks.content: Curl the tongue for ट ठ ड ढ ण. Keep the tongue forward for त थ द ध न. Practice slowly: तल (floor) vs टाल (postpone).

title: Pronunciation of Anusvar (अनुस्वार) in Hindi
url: Pronunciation of Anusvar (अनुस्वार) in Hindi
content: Anusvār (अनुस्वार) ं nasalizes the vowel; before velars it sounds like ङ, before dentals like न, etc.
intro: In Hindi, ं often indicates nasalization (गंगा, संगीत) or homorganic nasal before a stop (अंक, लंबा).

title: Combining consonants in Hindi (हिन्दी जोडाक्षरे)
url: Combining consonants in Hindi (हिन्दी जोडाक्षरे)
content: जोड़ाक्षर: use halant (्) to drop the inherent vowel so consonants join — क् + त = क्त, स् + क = स्क.
intro: Common clusters appear in स्कूल, प्रेम, स्त्री, अस्तित्व. Practice reading words left to right, one cluster at a time.

blocks.heading: Sentences with conjuncts
rows: हम शिक्षकों का सम्मान करते हैं
ham śikṣkõ kā sammān karte haiṃ

title: Combining consonants with र (r) – Hindi जोडाक्षरे Part 2
url: Combining consonants with र (r) – Hindi जोडाक्षरे Part 2
content: र् (reph) sits above the line: र्क, र्म, र्प. Subscript र appears in क्र, प्र, त्र, ग्र, द्र.
intro: त्र is common (country, three). प्र begins many Sanskrit-Hindi words. Practice the flap र clearly.

title: Combining consonants with ह (h) – Hindi जोडाक्षरे Part 3
url: Combining consonants with ह (h) – Hindi जोडाक्षरे Part 3
content: Aspirates (ख, घ, छ, झ, थ, ध, फ, भ) use extra breath; ह combines in ह्न, ह्म, ह्व and appears in धन्यवाद, बहुत.
intro: Practice aspirated stops: compare क-ख, ग-घ, च-छ, ट-ठ, त-थ, प-फ.

title: Special or separate symbols for combined consonants in Hindi
url: Special or separate symbols for combined consonants in Hindi
content: Special ligatures: क्ष (kṣa), त्र (tra), ज्ञ (jña) have fixed shapes; also ड़ ढ़ for flapped sounds in Hindi.
intro: क्ष appears in क्षमा, परक्षेत्र; त्र in त्रि, देश; ज्ञ in ज्ञान, योग्य.

title: Pronouns and Articles in Hindi
url: Pronouns and Articles in Hindi
content: Personal pronouns (मैं, हम, तुम, आप, वह, वे) and demonstratives (यह, वह); no indefinite article.
intro: आप is polite 'you'; तुम is informal. यह/वह agree with what you point at.

blocks.heading: Quick guide
blocks.content: Verbs agree with the subject in gender and number. Use आप + plural verb forms for respect.

title: Using plurals to indicate respect in Hindi
url: Using plurals to indicate respect in Hindi
content: Plural verb forms and plural pronouns (आप लोग, वे लोग) often mark respect, even when speaking to one person.
intro: In Hindi, honorific address may use plural agreement: आप कहाँ जा रहे हैं?

blocks.heading: Respect pattern
blocks.content: Use आप with plural auxiliary: हैं, करते हैं, जा रहे हैं — not *आप है.

blocks.heading: Respectful / plural-style sentences
rows: हम शिक्षकों का सम्मान करते हैं
ham śikṣkõ kā sammān karte haiṃ
क्या मैं आपकी मदद कर सकता हूँ?
kyā main āpkī madad kar saktā hū̃?
āp ke bacche acchā paṛhte haiṃ

title: Verbs in Hindi
url: Verbs in Hindi
content: Hindi verbs often end in -ना (infinitive): जाना, करना, खाना. Stem + tense endings carry agreement.
intro: Learn common roots first; tense markers (ता, रहा, गया) attach to the stem.

blocks.heading: Infinitives
blocks.content: Use करना 'to do', जाना 'to go', देखना 'to see', खाना 'to eat', पीना 'to drink', पढ़ना 'to read', सोना 'to sleep'.

title: Simple Present Tense in Hindi
url: Simple Present Tense in Hindi
content: Simple present for habits and facts: stem + ता/ती/ते + है/हैं (and हूँ for मैं).
intro: मैं जाता हूँ (m.), मैं जाती हूँ (f.); हम करते हैं; वह पढ़ता है / पढ़ती है.

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: Subject + verb stem + ता/ती/ते + है/हैं/हूँ according to gender and person.

blocks.heading: Habitual present
rows: हम लोगों की मदद करते हैं
ham logõ kī madad karte haiṃ
वह सावधानी से गाड़ी चलाता है
vah sāvdhānī se gāṛī calātā hai

title: Simple Present Tense of "To Be" in Hindi
url: Simple Present Tense of "To Be" in Hindi
content: Copula होना 'to be': है, हैं, हूँ, हो; negative नहीं है.
intro: मैं छात्र हूँ; तुम हो; वह है; वे हैं; यह किताब है.

blocks.heading: Forms
blocks.content: हूँ (1sg), हो (2sg informal), है (3sg), हैं (2 polite & plural, 3pl); past: था थी थे थीं.

title: Present Continuous Tense in Hindi
url: Present Continuous Tense in Hindi
content: Ongoing action: stem + रहा / रही / रहे + है / हैं / हूँ.
intro: मैं पढ़ रहा हूँ; वे खेल रहे हैं; बारिश हो रही है.

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: रह + gender ending + auxiliary for progressive aspect.

title: Simple Future Tense in Hindi
url: Simple Future Tense in Hindi
content: Future: गा / गी / गे with होगा / होगी / होगे — or simple future with -एगा / -एगी / -एगे on stem.
intro: मैं जाऊँगा; वह आएगी; हम करेंगे.

blocks.heading: Common future
blocks.content: Spoken Hindi often uses -गा series: मैं खाऊँगा, तुम जाओगे, वह लिखेगी.

title: Future Continuous Tense in Hindi
url: Future Continuous Tense in Hindi
content: Future progressive: रहा होगा / रही होगी / रहे होंगे — action will be in progress.
intro: तब तक मैं पढ़ रहा होऊँगा — by then I will be studying.

blocks.heading: Usage
blocks.content: Often for arrangements or predicted ongoing states at a future time.

blocks.heading: Future continuous
rows: By evening I will be free
शाम तक मैं फुरसत में होऊँगा
śām tak main fursat mẽ hoū̃gā
Tomorrow this time I will be flying
कल इस समय मैं उड़ रहा होऊँगा
kal is samay main uṛ rahā hoū̃gā
मैं आपको फोन कर रहा होऊँगा
main āpko phon kar rahā hoū̃gā

title: Simple Past Tense in Hindi – Part 1 (Verbs without object)
url: Simple Past Tense in Hindi – Part 1 (Verbs without object)
content: Past intransitive / without explicit object: गया / गई / गए + agreement; sometimes simple past stem.
intro: मैं गया; वह आई; हम सो गए.

blocks.heading: Part 1
blocks.content: Many verbs use perfective participle + auxiliary in past narrative: चला गया, आ गई.

title: Simple Past Tense in Hindi – Part 2 (Verbs with object)
url: Simple Past Tense in Hindi – Part 2 (Verbs with object)
content: With transitive verbs in past: agent takes ने and verb agrees with object: मैंने किताब पढ़ी.
intro: ने marks the doer; main verb often past participle agreeing with object.

blocks.heading: ने construction
blocks.content: मैंने खाना खाया; उसने पत्र लिखा; हमने फ़िल्म देखी.

title: Simple Past Tense in Hindi – Part 3 (Past tense of "to be")
url: Simple Past Tense in Hindi – Part 3 (Past tense of "to be")
content: Past of होना: था (m.sg), थी (f.sg), थे (m.pl / polite), थीं (f.pl).
intro: मैं थका हुआ था; वह बीमार थी; हम खुश थे.

blocks.heading: Negation
blocks.content: नहीं था / नहीं थी — was not.

title: Exceptional Verbs in Hindi which change in past tense form
url: Exceptional Verbs in Hindi which change in past tense form
content: Some verbs have suppletive or irregular past stems: गया from जाना, आया from आना, दिया from देना, लिया from लेना.
intro: Memorize common pairs; patterns differ slightly in perfective.

blocks.heading: Examples
blocks.content: गया/गई/गए; आया/आई/आए; दिया/दी/दिए; लिया/ली/लिए.

title: Past Continuous Tense in Hindi
url: Past Continuous Tense in Hindi
content: Past continuous: was/were + -ing → रहा था / रही थी / रहे थे.
intro: मैं पढ़ रहा था; बारिश हो रही थी.

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: Progressive participle + past tense of होना.

title: Present/Past/Future Perfect Tense in Hindi
url: Present/Past/Future Perfect Tense in Hindi
content: Perfect: चुका / चुकी / चुके + है/हैं/था — completed action with present/past auxiliary.
intro: मैं खा चुका हूँ; वह जा चुकी थी.

blocks.heading: Also
blocks.content: ले लिया, कर दिया express completed 'completive' aspect.

blocks.heading: Perfect tense
rows: By then I will have left
तब तक मैं जा चुका होऊँगा
tab tak main jā cukā hoū̃gā

title: Learn Prepositions in Hindi
url: Learn Prepositions in Hindi
content: Postpositions (so-called prepositions): में, पर, से, को, के लिए, के पास — noun + postposition.
intro: Hindi places relation words after the noun: घर में, मेज़ पर, दिल्ली से.

blocks.heading: Core set
blocks.content: को marks direct/indirect object in many frames; से 'from/with/by'; में 'in/at'; पर 'on/at'.

title: Preposition "TO" in Hindi
url: Preposition "TO" in Hindi
content: English 'to' (direction, recipient, purpose) often maps to को, में, की ओर, or infinitive + के लिए.
intro: मैं दिल्ली जा रहा हूँ; उसे पत्र लिखो; यह तुम्हारे लिए है.

blocks.heading: Direction vs dative
blocks.content: Place names: दिल्ली जाना. Person as goal: उसको देना. Purpose: पढ़ने के लिए.

blocks.heading: ‘To’ in Hindi
rows: यह पत्र पिताजी के लिए है
yah patr pitājī ke lie hai
मैंने मदद करने का वादा किया
mainne madad karne kā vādā kiyā

title: Sentences with person or living things as object in Hindi
url: Sentences with person or living things as object in Hindi
content: Animate objects take को: मैंने उसे देखा. Some verbs use से for person-source.
intro: को marks the person affected; contrast with inanimate direct objects in ने-constructions.

blocks.heading: People as objects
blocks.content: उसको बुलाओ; मैं तुम्हें जानता हूँ; हम उन्हें मिलेंगे.

blocks.heading: Person / animate object
rows: हम आप पर भरोसा करते हैं
ham āp par bharosā karte haiṃ

title: Saying My/His/Her in Hindi
url: Saying My/His/Her in Hindi
content: Possessive: मेरा/मेरी/मेरे, तुम्हारा, आपका, उसका, हमारा — agree with the thing possessed.
intro: मेरी किताब; मेरा घर; मेरे दोस्त.

blocks.heading: Agreement
blocks.content: Possessive adjective matches gender/number of the possessed noun, not always the owner.

title: Basic questions and "WH" questions in Hindi
url: Basic questions and "WH" questions in Hindi
content: Yes/no: क्या …? Tag questions: ना?, है ना? WH: कौन, क्या, कब, कहाँ, क्यों, कैसे.
intro: क्या आप ठीक हैं? आप कहाँ रहते हैं?

blocks.heading: Politeness
blocks.content: Use आप + plural verb in questions to strangers and elders.

blocks.heading: Questions
rows: क्या मैं कुछ पूछ सकता हूँ?
kyā main kuch pūch saktā hū̃?

title: Negative Sentences – Present tense in Hindi
url: Negative Sentences – Present tense in Hindi
content: Negation with नहीं before verb; नहीं है for 'is not'; मत for imperative 'don't'.
intro: मैं नहीं जा रहा; यह सच नहीं है.

blocks.heading: Present
blocks.content: नहीं + finite verb; sometimes न … नहीं for emphasis.

title: Negative Sentences – Past tense in Hindi
url: Negative Sentences – Past tense in Hindi
content: Past negation: नहीं + past form; नहीं था for 'was not'; ने … नहीं for completed actions.
intro: मैं नहीं गया; उसने नहीं देखा.

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: Same placement as present: verb carries negation.

title: Negative Sentence – Future Tense in Hindi
url: Negative Sentence – Future Tense in Hindi
content: Future negation: नहीं + future verb; sometimes मत + future for strong refusal in speech.
intro: मैं नहीं जाऊँगा; वह नहीं करेगी.

blocks.heading: Will not
blocks.content: नहीं before the future auxiliary or -गा form.

title: Working with nouns – Gender & Plurals in Hindi
url: Working with nouns – Gender & Plurals in Hindi
content: Nouns have gender (लिंग): masculine/feminine; plurals often -ए / ओं / याँ; learn patterns with common words.
intro: लड़का → लड़के; लड़की → लड़कियाँ; किताब → किताबें.

blocks.heading: Agreement
blocks.content: Adjectives and verbs often agree with noun gender/number.

title: Working with nouns – Prepositions in Hindi
url: Working with nouns – Prepositions in Hindi
content: Noun + postposition frames: के साथ, के बिना, के बाद, के पहले, में, पर, से.
intro: घर के बाद; दोस्त के साथ; काम पर.

blocks.heading: Chunks
blocks.content: Learn fixed three-word chains: नाम के लिए, समय के साथ.

blocks.heading: Nouns with postpositions
rows: मैं रात में काम करता हूँ
main rāt mẽ kām kartā hū̃
हम योग के बाद खाते हैं
ham yog ke bād khāte haiṃ
vah mujh se pahle calā gayā

title: Prepositions with similar pronunciation or similar meanings in Hindi
url: Prepositions with similar pronunciation or similar meanings in Hindi
content: Near-homophones: पर vs पर (stress), में vs मैं in speech; से vs सै in spelling.
intro: Listen: मैं घर में हूँ — मैं ≠ में.

blocks.heading: Meaning pairs
blocks.content: ऊपर 'above' vs ओपर 'upper' (rare); किनारा 'edge' vs किनारा place names — context.

title: Cases in Hindi (विभक्ति)
url: Cases in Hindi (विभक्ति)
content: विभक्ति: direct को; instrumental/ablative से; genitive का/की/के; locative में/पर.
intro: राम ने सीता को फल दिया — ने ergative, को object, का possession.

blocks.heading: Spoken Hindi
blocks.content: Postpositions do the job of Sanskrit cases; learn frames, not Sanskrit names.

blocks.heading: Cases (overview)
rows: राम ने सीता को फल दिया
rām ne sītā ko phal diyā

title: Commands / Imperative statements in Hindi
url: Commands / Imperative statements in Hindi
content: Imperatives: करो (informal), कीजिए (polite), मत करो 'don't'.
intro: आइए, बैठिए, देखिए — polite request forms.

blocks.heading: Levels
blocks.content: तुम करो; आप कीजिए; sometimes चलो for 'let's'.

title: Time related words in Hindi
url: Time related words in Hindi
content: Time words: आज, कल, परसों, सुबह, शाम, बजे, घंटा, मिनट, हफ़्ता, महीना, साल.
intro: सुबह सात बजे; शाम को; अगले हफ़्ते.

blocks.heading: Usage
blocks.content: कल = yesterday or tomorrow by context; परसों = day after tomorrow / before.

title: Verbs indicating cause of action (Causative verb) in Hindi
url: Verbs indicating cause of action (Causative verb) in Hindi
content: Causative: 'get X done' — बनवाना, पढ़वाना, दिखलाना; मैंने उससे काम करवाया.
intro: Double verbs add the sense of causing or having someone act.

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: Root + वा + ना: करवाना 'to get done', पढ़वाना 'to get taught'.

title: Gender-number agreement rule in Hindi
url: Gender-number agreement rule in Hindi
content: Agreement: verb/adjective match subject in intransitive clauses; with ने, verb may agree with object.
intro: लड़की गई; लड़के गए; मैंने किताब पढ़ी.

blocks.heading: ने rule
blocks.content: Transitive past: agent + ने + verb agreeing with object gender/number.

title: Past/Present/Future Perfect Tense – Second Style in Hindi
url: Past/Present/Future Perfect Tense – Second Style in Hindi
content: Second-style perfect: हो चुका है; or compound with रहा: पढ़ रहा हूँ — bookish/emphatic variants.
intro: Formal news: किया जा चुका है 'has been done'.

blocks.heading: Note
blocks.content: Spoken Hindi prefers चुका; written Hindi may use गया/आया compounds similarly.

blocks.heading: Perfect — second style
rows: मुझे सूचित कर दिया गया है
mujhe sūcit kar diyā gayā hai
वह पहले ही खा चुकी थी
vah pahle hī khā cukī thī
By then I will have finished
तब तक मैं खत्म कर चुका होऊँगा
tab tak main khatm kar cukā hoū̃gā

title: Past/Present/Future Perfect Continuous tense in Hindi
url: Past/Present/Future Perfect Continuous tense in Hindi
content: Perfect continuous: रहा हूँ / रहा था with 'for/since' time: दो घंटे से पढ़ रहा हूँ.
intro: Emphasizes duration up to now/then.

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: Time phrase + से + progressive + auxiliary.

blocks.heading: Perfect continuous
rows: I have been reading for two hours
मैं दो घंटे से पढ़ रहा हूँ
main do ghaṃṭe se paṛh rahā hū̃
वह गा रही है काफ़ी देर से
vah gā rahī hai kāfī der se
They have been working since morning
वे सुबह से काम कर रहे हैं
ve subah se kām kar rahe haiṃ
We will have been living here
She has been teaching for years
वह सालों से पढ़ा रही है
vah sālõ se paṛhā rahī hai
वह गायब है कुछ दिनों से
vah gāyab hai kuch dinõ se
I have been trying to call
मैं फोन करने की कोशिश कर रहा हूँ
main phon karne kī kośiś kar rahā hū̃
By May I will have been working
मई तक मैं काम कर रहा होऊँगा
maī tak main kām kar rahā hoū̃gā

title: Difference between "no/not" and "don't want" in Hindi
url: Difference between "no/not" and "don't want" in Hindi
content: नहीं negates fact; नहीं चाहिए / मन नहीं is 'don't want'; मत is imperative 'don't'.
intro: मुझे चाय नहीं चाहिए — I don't want tea. यह सच नहीं — it's not true.

blocks.heading: Contrast
blocks.content: नहीं खाता = does not eat; खाना नहीं चाहता = does not want to eat.

blocks.heading: no / not vs don't want
rows: He does not want to come
She did not want to ask
I don't want you to go
मैं नहीं चाहता कि तुम जाओ
main nahī̃ cāhtā ki tum jāo

title: Sentence using "Going to" phrase in Hindi
url: Sentence using "Going to" phrase in Hindi
content: Near future and intention: जा रहा हूँ, वाला है, करने वाला हूँ — English ‘going to’.
intro: Spoken Hindi uses progressive + जाना or वाला + noun/verb for planned action.

blocks.heading: Patterns
blocks.content: मैं अभी निकल रहा हूँ = I’m going (to leave). कल जाना है = have to / will go.

blocks.heading: Going to / about to
rows: I am going to leave now
I am going to Delhi tomorrow
मैं कल दिल्ली जा रहा हूँ
main kal dillī jā rahā hū̃
We are not going to wait
I was going to tell you
What are you going to do?
The train is about to leave
I am going for a walk

title: Sentence using "Used to" phrase in Hindi
url: Sentence using "Used to" phrase in Hindi
content: Habitual past: करता था / करती थी / करते थे — ‘used to’.
intro: Also: पहले … हुआ करता था for ‘there used to be’.

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: Imperfect past + था/थी/थे marks repeated or former habit.

blocks.heading: Used to
rows: ve ham se milne āte the
There used to be a well
पहले यहाँ कुआँ हुआ करता था
pahle yahā̃ kuā̃ huā kartā thā
We used to walk to school
I did not use to like it
मुझे यह पहले पसंद नहीं था
mujhe yah pahle pasand nahī̃ thā
वह रात में काम करता था
vah rāt mẽ kām kartā thā
The bus used to stop here

title: Sentence using "Used to" phrase – old style in Hindi
url: Sentence using "Used to" phrase – old style in Hindi
content: Older/literary habit: करा करते थे, हुआ करता था — same ‘used to’, more narrative tone.
intro: Common in stories: खाया करते थे, देखा करती थी.

blocks.heading: Style
blocks.content: Verb stem + आ / या + करना + past imperfect — marks repeated past action.

blocks.heading: Old-style ‘used to’
rows: वह ज़ोर से पढ़ा करते थे
vah zor se paṛhā karte the
हम अंधेरे से डरा करते थे
ham andhere se ḍarā karte the
She used to sing in temple
वह मंदिर में गाया करती थी
vah mandir mẽ gāyā kartī thī

title: Sentence using "If-Then" in Hindi
url: Sentence using "If-Then" in Hindi
content: Conditionals: अगर / यदि … तो / तो फिर — ‘if … then’.
intro: Spoken Hindi prefers अगर; तो can be omitted in quick speech.

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: अगर तुम आओगे, तो हम खाएँगे — if you come, we will eat.

blocks.heading: If–then
rows: If it rains, we will stay
अगर बारिश हुई तो हम रुकेंगे
agar bāriś huī to ham rukeṅge
If you are free, call me
अगर तुम फुरसत में हो तो फोन करना
agar tum fursat mẽ ho to phon karnā
अगर वह आए तो मुझे बताना
agar vah ā.e to mujhe batānā
If I knew, I would say
अगर मुझे पता होता तो मैं कहता
agar mujhe patā hotā to main kahtā
अगर वह माने तो शुरू करते हैं
agar vah māne to śurū karte haiṃ
If I am wrong, correct me
अगर मैं गलत हूँ तो सुधारो
agar main galat hū̃ to sudhāro
अगर वे आएं तो स्वागत करो
agar ve āeṃ to svāgat karo

title: Prepositions with Verbs in Hindi
url: Prepositions with Verbs in Hindi
content: Verb + postposition: ध्यान देना ‘pay attention to’, इंतज़ार करना ‘wait for’, विश्वास करना ‘trust’.
intro: The postposition (पर, में, का, से) is fixed with each verb.

blocks.heading: Learn chunks
blocks.content: इंतज़ार + का: किताब का इंतज़ार — wait for the book.

blocks.heading: Verbs with postpositions
rows: मैं आपका इंतज़ार कर रहा हूँ
main āpkā intazār kar rahā hū̃
वह मेहनत पर विश्वास करती है
vah mehnat par viśvās kartī hai
hamne is ke bāre mẽ socā

title: Adjectives in Hindi
url: Adjectives in Hindi
content: Adjectives agree in gender/number: बड़ा / बड़ी / बड़े; comparative often with से.
intro: Attributive: अच्छी किताब; predicative: किताब अच्छी है.

blocks.heading: Basics
blocks.content: Color, size, quality — place before noun or use with है.

title: Using verb "Can/To be able to" in Hindi
url: Using verb "Can/To be able to" in Hindi
content: Ability: सकना conjugated — कर सकता हूँ; ‘can’ for permission often क्या … सकता हूँ?
intro: नहीं सकता = cannot (ability); मत = don’t (permission).

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: Infinitive + सकना: पढ़ सकते हैं — can read.

blocks.heading: Can / be able to
rows: क्या आप मदद कर सकते हैं?
kyā āp madad kar sakte haiṃ?
I will be able to finish
मैं आपकी आवाज़ नहीं सुन सकता
main āpkī āvāz nahī̃ sun saktā
क्या मैं यहाँ बैठ सकता हूँ?
kyā main yahā̃ baiṭh saktā hū̃?
हम फिर कोशिश कर सकते हैं
ham phir kośiś kar sakte haiṃ

title: Using verb To Want/To Need in Hindi
url: Using verb To Want/To Need in Hindi
content: Need: चाहिए (invariable); want: चाहना — मुझे पानी चाहिए, मैं जाना चाहता हूँ.
intro: चाहिए follows dative subject: हमें आराम चाहिए.

blocks.heading: Contrast
blocks.content: मुझे खाना चाहिए = I need / should have food. मैं खाना चाहता हूँ = I want to eat.

blocks.heading: Want / need
rows: वह चाहती है कि वह रुके
vah cāhtī hai ki vah ruke

title: Verb "To Become/To Happen" in Hindi
url: Verb "To Become/To Happen" in Hindi
content: Become: बन जाना, हो जाना; happen: होना, घटित होना.
intro: मौसम ठंडा हो गया — the weather became cold.

blocks.heading: Usage
blocks.content: Adjective + हो जाना; profession + बनना: डॉक्टर बनना.

title: Using "Should" in Hindi
url: Using "Should" in Hindi
content: Soft obligation: चाहिए / अच्छा होगा / …ना चाहिए — ‘should’.
intro: Advice: आपको आराम करना चाहिए — you should rest.

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: Dative + verb + चाहिए; or अच्छा रहेगा for ‘it would be better’.

blocks.heading: Should
rows: It would be better to wait
बच्चों को बड़ों का सम्मान करना चाहिए
baccõ ko baṛõ kā sammān karnā cāhie
हमें समय बर्बाद नहीं करना चाहिए
hameṃ samay barbād nahī̃ karnā cāhie

title: Using "Must"/"to be have to" in Hindi
url: Using "Must"/"to be have to" in Hindi
content: Strong obligation: पड़ना ‘have to’, ज़रूरी है, अनिवार्य है — ‘must’.
intro: मुझे जाना पड़ा — I had to go.

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: Dative + infinitive + पड़ना; future पड़ेगा for ‘will have to’.

blocks.heading: Must / have to
rows: मुझे यह खत्म करना ही पड़ेगा
mujhe yah khatm karnā hī paṛegā
नियमों का पालन करना पड़ता है
niyamõ kā pālan karnā paṛtā hai

title: Using phrase "to keep doing" in Hindi
url: Using phrase "to keep doing" in Hindi
content: Continuative: करते रहना, जाते रहना — ‘keep (on) doing’.
intro: Also: लगे रहो — keep at it (imperative).

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: Present habitual stem + ते रहना for sustained action.

title: Comparison and degrees of comparison in Hindi
url: Comparison and degrees of comparison in Hindi
content: Comparison: A से B ज़्यादा; superlative: सबसे; equality: जितना … उतना.
intro: बड़ा, बड़ा, सबसे बड़ा — big, bigger, biggest (context + से).

blocks.heading: Degrees
blocks.content: यह मुझसे लंबा है — taller than me. सबसे सस्ता — cheapest.

blocks.heading: Comparison
rows: vah sab se tez dauṛtā hai

title: Using verb "To Know" in Hindi
url: Using verb "To Know" in Hindi
content: Know facts: पता होना; know people/skills: जानना; ‘I know how’: आना.
intro: मुझे पता है; मैं उसे जानता हूँ; मुझे तैरना आता है.

blocks.heading: Frames
blocks.content: पता चलना = find out; मालूम होना = be known.

blocks.heading: To know
rows: मैं उसे अच्छी तरह जानता हूँ
main use acchī tarah jāntā hū̃

title: Using "Let" and "Shall we" in Hindi
url: Using "Let" and "Shall we" in Hindi
content: Let’s: चलो …; polite suggestion: चलिए; Shall we?: चलें? आगे बढ़ें?
intro: देखते हैं — let’s see (we’ll see).

blocks.heading: Hortative
blocks.content: चलो खाते हैं — let’s eat. मैं करने दूँ — let me do it.

blocks.heading: Let / Shall we
rows: क्या मैं अंदर आ सकता हूँ?
kyā main andar ā saktā hū̃?

title: Which-That / What-That sentences in Hindi
url: Which-That / What-That sentences in Hindi
content: Relative clauses: जो … वह/वे — ‘the one who …’ / ‘what …’.
intro: जो किताब मैंने पढ़ी वह अच्छी थी — the book that I read was good.

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: जो introduces the clause; correlative वह/वे picks up the head.

blocks.heading: Which-that / what-that
rows: जो तुमने कहा वह सच है
jo tumne kahā vah sac hai
मुझे पता है वह क्या चाहता है
mujhe patā hai vah kyā cāhtā hai
जो लोग यहाँ काम करते हैं
jo log yahā̃ kām karte haiṃ
मुझे याद है जहाँ हम मिले
mujhe yād hai jahā̃ ham mile
जो वह कहता है कुछ नहीं
jo vah kahtā hai kuch nahī̃
This is the same pen I lost
यह वही कलम है जो खो गई
yah vahī kalam hai jo kho gaī

title: Giving instructions formally in Hindi
url: Giving instructions formally in Hindi
content: Formal tone: कृपया + polite imperative; passive-style: किया जाए; निर्देशानुसार — as instructed.
intro: Documents: उपरोक्त अनुसार पालन करें — comply as above.

blocks.heading: Register
blocks.content: कृपया + verb + इए; कृपया ध्यान दें — please note.

blocks.heading: Formal instructions
rows: किसी प्रश्न के लिए संपर्क करें
kisī praśn ke lie sampark kareṃ

title: Using "To Like" in Hindi
url: Using "To Like" in Hindi
content: Liking: पसंद है + dative subject; पसंद करना for ‘to like (doing)’.
intro: मुझे संगीत पसंद है — I like music.

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: मुझे चाय पसंद नहीं — I don’t like tea.

blocks.heading: To like
rows: क्या आपको मसालेदार खाना पसंद है?
kyā āpko masāledār khānā pasand hai?
मुझे चाय कॉफ़ी से ज़्यादा पसंद है
mujhe cāy kŏfī se zyādā pasand hai
मुझे यह किताब बहुत पसंद है
mujhe yah kitāb bahut pasand hai

title: Sentences using "would" in Hindi
url: Sentences using "would" in Hindi
content: Habitual ‘would’: कहता था, जाते थे; polite ‘would like’: चाहूँगा; conditional: होता तो.
intro: मैं सोचता था = I would think (used to). मैं आऊँगा = I will come; मैं आना चाहूँगा = I would like to come.

blocks.heading: Soft requests
blocks.content: क्या आप मदद करेंगे? — would you help? (future polite).

blocks.heading: Would
rows: मुझे चाय चाहिए / मैं चाय लूँगा
If I were you, I would wait
मैं आपकी जगह होता तो इंतज़ार करता
main āpkī jagah hotā to intazār kartā

title: "To Understand" & "To come to know" in Hindi
url: "To Understand" & "To come to know" in Hindi
content: Understand: समझना; realize/learn: समझ आना, पता चलना — ‘come to know’.
intro: मुझे समझ आ गया — I understood / it dawned on me.

blocks.heading: Frames
blocks.content: बात समझ में आई — I got the point. मालूम पड़ा — I found out.

blocks.heading: Understand / come to know
rows: उसने समझाया जब तक मैं समझ न गया
usne samjhāyā jab tak main samajh na gayā
वह समझने का नाटक करता है
vah samajhne kā nāṭak kartā hai
हम एक दूसरे को समझते हैं
ham ek dūsre ko samajhte haiṃ

title: Add a question tag in Hindi
url: Add a question tag in Hindi
content: Tags: ना?, है ना?, नहीं? — seeking confirmation.
intro: तुम आओगे ना? — you’ll come, right?

blocks.heading: Tone
blocks.content: है ना softens statements: अच्छा है ना — it’s nice, isn’t it.

blocks.heading: Question tags
rows: वह गाड़ी चला लेती है ना?
vah gāṛī calā letī hai nā?

title: Using verb "To remember" in Hindi
url: Using verb "To remember" in Hindi
content: Remember: याद रखना; forget: भूल जाना; remind: याद दिलाना.
intro: मुझे याद है — I remember; मुझे भूल गया — I forgot.

blocks.heading: Frames
blocks.content: याद आना = to come to mind; याद दिलाना = remind.

blocks.heading: To remember
rows: This song reminds me of home
यह गाना मुझे घर की याद दिलाता है
yah gānā mujhe ghar kī yād dilātā hai
Write it down or you’ll forget
मुझे भूल गया उसने क्या कहा
mujhe bhūl gayā usne kyā kahā
मुझे याद है मैंने उसे देखा
mujhe yād hai mainne use dekhā

title: Using "To Want"/"To Need" with other verbs in Hindi
url: Using "To Want"/"To Need" with other verbs in Hindi
content: Want + infinitive: जाना चाहता हूँ; need + infinitive: जाना पड़ेगा.
intro: चाहती है कि मैं जाऊँ — she wants me to go.

blocks.heading: Chains
blocks.content: मैं खाना बनाना चाहता हूँ — I want to cook.

blocks.heading: Want/need + other verbs
rows: वह आने से इनकार करती है
vah āne se inkār kartī hai
मैं मदद करने को तैयार हूँ
main madad karne ko taiyār hū̃
वह जानने का नाटक करता है
vah jānn kā nāṭak kartā hai
हम जीतने की आशा रखते हैं
ham jītne kī āśā rakhte haiṃ
मुझे आज ही खत्म करना है
mujhe āj hī khatm karnā hai
वे चाहते हैं कि हम इंतज़ार करें
ve cāhte haiṃ ki ham intazār kareṃ

title: Using "To Want" with other person in Hindi
url: Using "To Want" with other person in Hindi
content: Want for another person: चाहता है कि + clause; or dative: मैं चाहता हूँ कि वह आए.
intro: माँ चाहती हैं कि मैं पढ़ूँ — mother wants me to study.

blocks.heading: Subjunctive-style
blocks.content: कि introduces what someone wants someone else to do.

blocks.heading: Want (other person)
rows: मैं चाहता हूँ कि तुम रुको
main cāhtā hū̃ ki tum ruko
वह चाहती है कि वह फोन करे
vah cāhtī hai ki vah phon kare
हम चाहते हैं वे मान जाएँ
ham cāhte haiṃ ve mān jāeṃ
He doesn’t want me to go
वे चाहते हैं हम इंतज़ार करें
ve cāhte haiṃ ham intazār kareṃ
हम चाहते हैं सब सुरक्षित रहें
ham cāhte haiṃ sab surakṣit raheṃ

title: Using phrase "supposed to" in Hindi
url: Using phrase "supposed to" in Hindi
content: Expectation: होना चाहिए tha — was supposed to; नियत था — planned.
intro: मुझे वहाँ होना चाहिए था — I was supposed to be there.

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: चाहिए था for unmet expectation; वाला था for ‘was going to’.

blocks.heading: Supposed to
rows: The train was supposed to arrive
We are supposed to follow this
What was I supposed to do?
You’re not supposed to park here
The meeting is supposed to start
I thought it was supposed to be easy
Nobody told me I was supposed to come
किसी ने नहीं कहा मुझे आना था
kisī ne nahī̃ kahā mujhe ānā thā

title: Using "To Like" with other verbs in Hindi
url: Using "To Like" with other verbs in Hindi
content: Like + gerund: पसंद है … करना; enjoy doing: मज़ा आना.
intro: मुझे दौड़ना पसंद है — I like running.

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: Infinitive as noun: पढ़ना पसंद है.

blocks.heading: Like + verbs
rows: उसे दोस्तों के लिए खाना बनाना पसंद है
use dostõ ke lie khānā banānā pasand hai
मुझे उपन्यास पढ़ने में मज़ा आता है
mujhe upanyās paṛhne mẽ mazā ātā hai
मुझे चलाना गाड़ी चलाने से ज़्यादा पसंद है
mujhe calānā gāṛī calāne se zyādā pasand hai
हमें वह फ़िल्म देखना पसंद आया
hameṃ vah film dekhna pasand āyā
क्या आपको यहाँ काम करना पसंद है?
kyā āpko yahā̃ kām karnā pasand hai?
She doesn’t like getting up early
हमें आपकी कहानियाँ सुनना पसंद है
hameṃ āpkī kahāniā̃ sunnā pasand hai

title: Verbs which use preposition "To" with subject and verb form as per object in Hindi
url: Verbs which use preposition "To" with subject and verb form as per object in Hindi
content: Psychological verbs + को: समझ आना, पसंद आना, सूझना — experiencer subject often dative.
intro: मुझे यह पसंद आया — I liked it (it pleased me).

blocks.heading: Agreement
blocks.content: Sometimes the stimulus is subject: फ़िल्म अच्छी लगी.

blocks.heading: Verb + to (experiencer)
rows: मुझे कुत्तों से डर लगता है
mujhe kuttõ se ḍar lagtā hai

title: Short forms in spoken Hindi
url: Short forms in spoken Hindi
content: Colloquial shortenings: वो, फिर, अभी, बस, हाँ, ना; dropping मैं in fast speech.
intro: Spoken: मैंने→often मैं ने slurred; वह→वो.

blocks.heading: Tips
blocks.content: Learn to recognize वो, ऐसे, फिर भी in conversation.

title: Greeting, Wishes, Blessings, Slogans in Hindi
url: Greeting, Wishes, Blessings, Slogans in Hindi
content: नमस्ते, नमस्कार; शुभकामनाएँ; जन्मदिन मुबारक; जय हिन्द — greetings and slogans.
intro: Time-based: सुप्रभात, शुभ रात्रि.

blocks.heading: Occasions
blocks.content: शादी की बधाई — wedding wishes; ईद मुबारक — festival greetings.

title: Compound verbs / Phrases in Hindi
url: Compound verbs / Phrases in Hindi
content: Light verbs: देना, लेना, जाना with main verb — कर देना, उठ लेना, चला जाना.
intro: Adds nuance: completion, benefit, movement.

blocks.heading: Examples
blocks.content: पढ़ लेना ‘read through’; खा लेना ‘eat up’; देख लेना ‘take a look’.

title: Calling or Addressing someone in Hindi
url: Calling or Addressing someone in Hindi
content: Titles: जी, साहब, जी; family: भैया, दीदी, चाचा; respectful: श्री, श्रीमती.
intro: आप + plural verb when addressing elders.

blocks.heading: Politeness
blocks.content: Teacher: अध्यापक जी; stranger: भैया/बहन.

title: Exclamations in Hindi
url: Exclamations in Hindi
content: Exclamations: अरे!, वाह!, हाय!, शाबाश!, अफ़सोस! — surprise, praise, pity.
intro: ओहो for mild surprise; अच्छा for ‘oh I see’.

blocks.heading: Intensity
blocks.content: अरे यार — friendly frustration.

title: Requesting someone – "May I", "Can you" etc. in Hindi
url: Requesting someone – "May I", "Can you" etc. in Hindi
content: Polite requests: कृपया; क्या मैं …?; क्या आप … सकते हैं? — May I / Can you.
intro: मेरी एक विनती है — I have a humble request.

blocks.heading: Softeners
blocks.content: ज़रा … दीजिए — please give a little …

blocks.heading: Requests
rows: क्या मैं अंदर आ सकता हूँ?
kyā main andar ā saktā hū̃?
क्या आप मेरी मदद कर सकते हैं?
kyā āp merī madad kar sakte haiṃ?
क्या आप थोड़ा इंतज़ार कर सकते हैं?
kyā āp thoṛā intazār kar sakte haiṃ?
क्या मैं कुछ पूछ सकता हूँ?
kyā main kuch pūch saktā hū̃?
क्या मैं बाद में भर सकता हूँ?
kyā main bād mẽ bhar saktā hū̃?
क्या आप यह देख सकते हैं?
kyā āp yah dekh sakte haiṃ?
क्या आप टैक्सी बुला सकते हैं?
kyā āp ṭaiksī bulā sakte haiṃ?
क्या हम यहाँ बैठ सकते हैं?
kyā ham yahā̃ baiṭh sakte haiṃ?
क्या आप धीरे बोल सकते हैं?
kyā āp dhīre bol sakte haiṃ?

title: Showing uncertainty using May, Might in Hindi
url: Showing uncertainty using May, Might in Hindi
content: Uncertainty: शायद, हो सकता है, शक है — may / might.
intro: मुझे लगता है … for soft belief.

blocks.heading: Hedging
blocks.content: शायद बारिश हो — it might rain.

blocks.heading: May / might
rows: वह देर से आ सकती है
vah der se ā saktī hai
हो सकता है हम रद्द करें
ho saktā hai ham radd kareṃ

title: Adjectives made from verb in Hindi
url: Adjectives made from verb in Hindi
content: Participial adjectives: खाया हुआ, बना हुआ, लिखा हुआ — eaten, ready-made, written.
intro: Often before noun: ताज़ा कटा हुआ सलाद.

blocks.heading: Pattern
blocks.content: Past participle + हुआ agrees with noun.

title: Adjectives related to prepositions in Hindi
url: Adjectives related to prepositions in Hindi
content: Locative compounds as adjectives: ऊपर वाला ‘upper’, अंदर का ‘inner’, बाहर का ‘outer’.
intro: पीछे वाला कमरा — the room at the back.

blocks.heading: Usage
blocks.content: वाला agrees: ऊपर वाली मंज़िल.

title: Using May to express wish in Hindi
url: Using May to express wish in Hindi
content: Wishes with भगवान करे, काश — May God … / I wish ….
intro: काश मैं वहाँ होता — I wish I were there.

blocks.heading: Blessings
blocks.content: ख़ुदा हाफ़िज़ — goodbye (God protect).

title: Phrase "To Make Someone do something" in Hindi
url: Phrase "To Make Someone do something" in Hindi
content: Causative: करवाना, बनवाना, दिखवाना — make/have someone do.
intro: मैंने उससे काम करवाया — I got the work done by him.

blocks.heading: Double causative
blocks.content: पढ़वाना ‘get taught’.

blocks.heading: Make someone do
rows: I will get him to sign

title: Generalizing words – somewhere, somehow, anything, anybody etc. in Hindi
url: Generalizing words – somewhere, somehow, anything, anybody etc. in Hindi
content: Indefinites: कहीं, किसी, कुछ, कोई — somewhere, someone, something, anyone.
intro: Negation: कुछ नहीं, कहीं नहीं.

blocks.heading: Any-
blocks.content: कोई भी, कुछ भी — anyone, anything (colloquial).

title: "To have" to indicate possession and relation in Hindi
url: "To have" to indicate possession and relation in Hindi
content: Possession: के पास है; relationship: का / की — मेरा भाई, मेरी माँ.
intro: मेरे पास समय नहीं — I don’t have time.

blocks.heading: Existential
blocks.content: घर में कार है — there is a car at home.

blocks.heading: Have (possession)
rows: kyā āp ke pās samay hai?
मेरे पास कहने को कुछ नहीं
mere pās kahne ko kuch nahī̃

title: Using "To mean". How to ask meaning in Hindi
url: Using "To mean". How to ask meaning in Hindi
content: Meaning: मतलब, अर्थ, मायने — ‘to mean’.
intro: क्या मतलब? — what do you mean? यह क्या है? — what is this called?

blocks.heading: Asking
blocks.content: इसका हिन्दी में क्या कहते हैं? — what’s this called in Hindi?

blocks.heading: To mean / ask meaning
rows: How do you say … in Hindi?
… को हिन्दी में क्या कहते हैं?
… ko hindī mẽ kyā kahte haiṃ?
X से मेरा मतलब Y है
X se merā matlab Y hai
Look it up in the dictionary

title: Conjunctions in Hindi – Part 1 (And, But, Because, So, That, Although, Though, Still, Yet)
url: Conjunctions in Hindi – Part 1 (And, But, Because, So, That, Although, Though, Still, Yet)
content: Part 1: और, लेकिन, क्योंकि, इसलिए, कि, हालाँकि, फिर भी, अभी तक.
intro: क्योंकि gives reason; इसलिए gives result.

blocks.heading: Pairs
blocks.content: हालाँकि … फिर भी — although … still.

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 1
rows: वह गरीब है लेकिन ईमानदार है
vah garīb hai lekin īmāndār hai
हालाँकि थका था, फिर भी काम किया
hālãki thakā thā, phir bhī kām kiyā
I think that it is true
मुझे लगता है कि यह सच है
mujhe lagtā hai ki yah sac hai
उसने कोशिश की फिर भी हार गई
usne kośiś kī phir bhī hār gaī

title: Conjunctions in Hindi – Part 2 (As, Or, Neither, Nor, If, in case, provided)
url: Conjunctions in Hindi – Part 2 (As, Or, Neither, Nor, If, in case, provided)
content: Part 2: जैसा, या, न तो … न, अगर, अगर … तो, यदि, शायद.
intro: Neither … nor: न तो … न.

blocks.heading: Either/or
blocks.content: या … या — either … or (emphatic).

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 2
rows: न … न / न तो … न भी
na … na / na to … na bhī
You may go provided you finish
जा सकते हो बशर्ते खत्म करो
jā sakte ho baśarte khatm karo

title: Conjunctions in Hindi – Part 3 (After, then, As far as, As long as, As if)
url: Conjunctions in Hindi – Part 3 (After, then, As far as, As long as, As if)
content: Part 3: जब, तब, फिर, जहाँ तक, जब तक, मानो.
intro: जब तक … तब तक — as long as / until.

blocks.heading: Sequence
blocks.content: पहले … फिर — first … then.

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 3
rows: जब वह आया तब मैं चला गया
jab vah āyā tab main calā gayā
जब तक मैं न आऊँ तब तक रुको
jab tak main na āū̃ tab tak ruko
He talks as if he knows
वह ऐसे बोलता है मानो सब जानता हो
vah aise boltā hai māno sab jāntā ho
As long as we are together
She sings as if in a dream
वह गाती है जैसे सपने में हो
vah gātī hai jaise sapne mẽ ho

title: Conjunctions in Hindi – Part 4 (As Soon as, Once, Rather Than, Instead of, So that)
url: Conjunctions in Hindi – Part 4 (As Soon as, Once, Rather Than, Instead of, So that)
content: Part 4: जैसे ही, एक बार, बजाय, के बजाय, ताकि.
intro: ताकि expresses purpose — so that.

blocks.heading: Rather than
blocks.content: के बजाय — instead of.

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 4
rows: एक बार शुरू किया तो रुको मत
ek bār śurū kiyā to ruko mat
फोन करो ताकि मुझे पता चले
phon karo tāki mujhe patā cale
He left so that we could talk
वह चला गया ताकि हम बात कर सकें
vah calā gayā tāki ham bāt kar sakeṃ

title: List of Conjunctions in Hindi
url: List of Conjunctions in Hindi
content: Quick reference list of Hindi connectors for reading and writing.
intro: Group by function: addition, contrast, cause, time, condition.

blocks.heading: Tip
blocks.content: Read news editorials to see these in long sentences.

title: Combining sentences in Hindi
url: Combining sentences in Hindi
content: Join clauses with shared subjects or conjunctive adverbs: फिर, इसलिए, इसके अलावा.
intro: Avoid comma splices; use और, लेकिन, क्योंकि.

blocks.heading: Writing
blocks.content: Short sentences + और for simple lists.

blocks.heading: Combining sentences
rows: I woke up and drank tea
वह थका है लेकिन खुश है
vah thakā hai lekin khush hai

title: Compound verbs in Hindi
url: Compound verbs in Hindi
content: Systematic compound verbs: aspectual light verbs (ले, दे, उठ, बैठ, रख, डाल).
intro: Contrast with lesson ‘Compound verbs / Phrases’ (572): here, grammar names and patterns.

blocks.heading: Drill
blocks.content: Same stem + different light verb = different nuance.

title: Active Passive voice in Hindi
url: Active Passive voice in Hindi
content: Passive: किया गया, देखा जाता है — agent optional with से.
intro: यह किया गया — this was done.

blocks.heading: Spoken preference
blocks.content: Active is common; passive in news and formal style.

title: Active Passive voice in Hindi – Style 2
url: Active Passive voice in Hindi – Style 2
content: Style 2: impersonal passives, जा सकता है, हो सकता है — ‘it can be done’.
intro: यहाँ धूम्रपान नहीं किया जा सकता.

blocks.heading: Modals + passive
blocks.content: Permission and possibility in formal signs.

blocks.heading: Passive style 2
rows: यह वापस नहीं किया जा सकता
yah vāpas nahī̃ kiyā jā saktā
टिकट ऑनलाइन बुक की जा सकती हैं
ṭikaṭ ônlāin buk kī jā saktī haiṃ
Payment shall be made in advance
Goods once sold will not be returned
बिका हुआ माल वापस नहीं होगा
bikā huā māl vāpas nahī̃ hogā

title: Using "To Feel" & "To think" in Hindi
url: Using "To Feel" & "To think" in Hindi
content: Feel: महसूस करना, लगना; think: सोचना, समझना, लगता है.
intro: मुझे ठंड लग रही है — I feel cold.

blocks.heading: Opinion
blocks.content: मुझे लगता है कि … — I think that …

blocks.heading: Feel & think
rows: मैं थका महसूस कर रहा हूँ
main thakā mahasūs kar rahā hū̃
मुझे लगता है आप सही हैं
mujhe lagtā hai āp sahī haiṃ
मुझे उस पर तरस आता है
mujhe us par taras ātā hai
मैं ईमानदारी में विश्वास करता हूँ
main īmāndārī mẽ viśvās kartā hū̃
क्या आप बेहतर महसूस कर रहे हैं?
kyā āp behtar mahasūs kar rahe haiṃ?
वह भविष्य के बारे में सोचती है
vah bhaviṣy ke bāre mẽ sochtī hai

title: Idioms and Phrases in Hindi
url: Idioms and Phrases in Hindi
content: Idioms: हाथ पर हाथ धरे बैठे रहना; आँख में धूल झोंकना — sit idle; deceive.
intro: Learn fixed images; meaning is not literal.

blocks.heading: Common
blocks.content: दिल से — wholeheartedly; मुँह पर हाथ रखना — be shocked.

blocks.heading: Idioms 1
rows: हाथ पर हाथ धरे बैठे रहना
hāth par hāth dhare baiṭhe rehnā
to cost an arm and a leg
समय पंख लगाकर उड़ जाता है
samay paṅkh lagākar uṛ jātā hai

title: Idioms and Phrases in Hindi – Part 2
url: Idioms and Phrases in Hindi – Part 2
content: More idioms: मुँह की खाना; पैर पीछे खींचना — eat one’s words; back out.
intro: Regional variants exist; stick to widely understood forms.

blocks.heading: Caution
blocks.content: Some idioms are informal; avoid in formal letters.

blocks.heading: Idioms 2
rows: to hit the nail on the head
to let the cat out of the bag
to have a chip on one’s shoulder
the ball is in your court

title: Sentences using "If-Then" Style 2 in Hindi
url: Sentences using "If-Then" Style 2 in Hindi
content: Style 2 conditionals: यदि … तो अन्यथा; नहीं तो — if not … then; otherwise.
intro: Formal यदि … अथवा — if … or else.

blocks.heading: Mixed
blocks.content: अगर तुम नहीं आओगे तो हम नहीं जाएँगे — conditional threat/promise.

blocks.heading: If–then style 2
rows: If you hurry, you will catch
अगर जल्दी करोगे तो पकड़ लोगे
agar jaldī karoge to pakṛ loge
नहीं तो हम रद्द कर देंगे
nahī̃ to ham radd kar deṃge
Unless you try, you won’t win
जब तक कोशिश न करोगे जीतोगे नहीं
jab tak kośiś na karoge jītoge nahī̃
मान लो वह मना कर दे
mān lo vah manā kar de
अगर सहमत हैं तो हस्ताक्षर करें
agar sahamat haiṃ to hastākṣar kareṃ

title: List of Body parts in Hindi
url: List of Body parts in Hindi
content: Common body-part nouns (singular); many pair with मेरा/मेरी for possession.
intro: Learn the word, then use in short sentences: मेरा सिर दर्द कर रहा है.

blocks.heading: Tip
blocks.content: Gender matters for possessives: मेरा हाथ (m.), मेरी आँख (f.).

title: Frequently used words – Miscellaneous in Hindi
url: Frequently used words – Miscellaneous in Hindi
content: High-frequency nouns and adjectives for daily talk: things, places, people, time.
intro: Use these in your own mini-sentences to remember them.

blocks.heading: Mix
blocks.content: These appear constantly in conversation and reading.

title: Relations in Hindi
url: Relations in Hindi
content: Family and relations; जी adds respect: माता जी, पिता जी.
intro: ससुराल — in-laws’ side; मायका — maternal home (common speech).

blocks.heading: Usage
blocks.content: Address with relation + जी to elders.

title: Numbers in Hindi – Part 1
url: Numbers in Hindi – Part 1
content: Cardinals 0–10 and teens; बीस, तीस for tens up to ninety in speech patterns.
intro: एक, दो, तीन … दस; ग्यारह … उन्नीस.

blocks.heading: Part 1
blocks.content: Spell aloud; pair with counting objects: तीन किताबें.

title: Numbers in Hindi – Part 2
url: Numbers in Hindi – Part 2
content: Tens, hundreds, thousands; लाख and करोड़ for large amounts.
intro: Read prices and years: दो हज़ार छब्बीस.

title: Fractional numbers, sequence, percentage in Hindi
url: Fractional numbers, sequence, percentage in Hindi
content: Fractions: आधा, चौथाई; sequence: पहला, दूसरा; percentage: प्रतिशत.
intro: तीन बटे चार — three fourths (in math style).

blocks.heading: Everyday
blocks.content: आधा किलो — half a kilo; पचास प्रतिशत — fifty percent.

title: Frequently used verbs in Hindi – Part 1
url: Frequently used verbs in Hindi – Part 1
content: Core verbs for daily actions: movement, basic transitives.
intro: Memorize infinitive + present stem pattern later; here, the citation form.

blocks.heading: Part 1
blocks.content: जाना, आना, करना, देना, लेना, खाना, पीना.

title: Frequently used verbs in Hindi – Part 2
url: Frequently used verbs in Hindi – Part 2
content: Verbs for learning, work, and communication.
intro: Part 2 builds on Part 1 for classroom and office contexts.

blocks.heading: Part 2
blocks.content: Use with ने objects where transitive: पढ़ना, लिखना.

title: Frequently used verbs in Hindi – Part 3
url: Frequently used verbs in Hindi – Part 3
content: Verbs of thought, feeling, and change; many pair with को or से.
intro: Part 3 for inner states and social verbs.

blocks.heading: Part 3
blocks.content: लगना, चाहिए, पसंद — often with dative experiencer.

title: Frequently used verbs in Hindi – Part 4
url: Frequently used verbs in Hindi – Part 4
content: Verbs for cleaning, fixing, technology, and travel.
intro: Part 4 rounds out household and modern life.

blocks.heading: Part 4
blocks.content: Many are noun + करना: फोन करना.

title: One verb, multiple meanings – लावब/देब in Hindi
url: One verb, multiple meanings – लावब/देब in Hindi
content: Hindi लाना ‘to bring’ and देना ‘to give’ — many compounds and senses (cf. Magahi/Maithili लावब / देब).
intro: लाना: physical bring + metaphorical ‘produce’; देना: give + permit + light-verb completion.

blocks.heading: लाना
blocks.content: लाना, ले आना — e.g. पैसा लाओ, किताब ले आओ (bring money, bring the book).

blocks.heading: देना
blocks.content: दे देना finish; बता देना tell; मार देना strike (idiomatic).

title: One verb, multiple meanings – लागब in Hindi
url: One verb, multiple meanings – लागब in Hindi
content: Hindi लगना — many senses: attach, suit, feel, seem, cost (cf. Magahi/Maithili लागब).
intro: Experiencer often dative: मुझे ठंड लग रही है.

blocks.heading: Common frames
blocks.content: लगना + को: time/schedule; लगना + पर: blame; लगना + की: seem.

blocks.heading: लगना — multiple meanings
rows: यह शर्ट आप पर अच्छी लगेगी
मुझे घर की याद लग रही है
mujhe ghar kī yād lag rahī hai

title: List of Hindi Adjectives – Part 1
url: List of Hindi Adjectives – Part 1
content: Common adjectives for size, quality, age, and judgment.
intro: Agree with noun: बड़ा लड़का, बड़ी लड़की.

blocks.heading: Part 1
blocks.content: नया/पुराना, अच्छा/बुरा — opposites in daily speech.

title: Adverbs in Hindi – Part 1
url: Adverbs in Hindi – Part 1
content: Adverbs of manner: how something happens — धीरे, जल्दी, अचानक.
intro: Often end in -े when modifying verbs: धीरे बोलो.

blocks.heading: Part 1
blocks.content: Place मैं/यहाँ; time अभी/कल with verbs.

title: List of adverbs in Hindi
url: List of adverbs in Hindi
content: More adverbs: degree, frequency, doubt, and sentence adverbs.
intro: बहुत, बिल्कुल, वाकई — intensity and emphasis.

blocks.heading: List
blocks.content: Stack after verb or before adjective: बहुत अच्छा.

title: List of vegetable names in Hindi
url: List of vegetable names in Hindi
content: Common vegetables on the plate and in the market.
intro: Gender varies: आलू (m.), भिंडी (f.); learn with measure: एक किलो टमाटर.

blocks.heading: Cooking
blocks.content: प्याज़, लहसुन, अदरक — base of many curries.

title: List of names of fruits in Hindi
url: List of names of fruits in Hindi
content: Fruit names; many loanwords are common in Hindi markets.
intro: सेब, केला — everyday; अनार, संतरा — seasonal favorites.

blocks.heading: Note
blocks.content: केला is banana; नारियल is coconut.

title: List of names of birds in Hindi
url: List of names of birds in Hindi
content: Common birds; some names are onomatopoeic or descriptive.
intro: कोयल — cuckoo; उल्लू — owl (also slang ‘fool’ in jokes).

blocks.heading: Culture
blocks.content: मोर is India’s national bird (peacock).

title: List of names of insects in Hindi
url: List of names of insects in Hindi
content: Insects and small creatures; household pests and nature.
intro: Careful: कुछ जहरीले हो सकते हैं — some can be poisonous.

blocks.heading: Home
blocks.content: मक्खी, मच्छर — very common complaints.

title: List of colours in Hindi
url: List of colours in Hindi
content: Basic colour words; combine with चमड़ा, कपड़ा: लाल रंग.
intro: गुलाबी, नारंगी — colours as adjectives before nouns.

blocks.heading: Agreement
blocks.content: लाल सेब, लाल किताबें — colour adjective often invariant in casual speech.

title: List of names of flowers in Hindi
url: List of names of flowers in Hindi
content: Flower names for gardens, worship, and festivals.
intro: गुलाब — rose; गेंदा — marigold (common in garlands).

blocks.heading: Festivals
blocks.content: फूलों की माला — flower garland.

title: List of names of animals in Hindi
url: List of names of animals in Hindi
content: Common wild and domestic animals.
intro: भालू — bear; शेर — lion/tiger context in stories.

blocks.heading: Farm
blocks.content: गाय, भैंस, बकरी — milk animals.

title: Frequently used or common words in Hindi – Miscellaneous
url: Frequently used or common words in Hindi – Miscellaneous
content: Extra high-frequency words: fillers, reactions, and handy nouns.
intro: Use with care: अच्छा as ‘okay/I see’ is extremely common.

blocks.heading: Misc
blocks.content: ठीक है, बस, वैसे — conversation glue.

title: Diwali Festival in Hindi
url: Diwali Festival in Hindi
content: Diwali: lights, sweets, family visits, and safe celebrations in natural Hindi.
intro: Read aloud; imagine greeting neighbours and shopping for diyas before the festival.

blocks.heading: Setting
blocks.content: दीवाली पर घर रोशन होता है और लोग एक दूसरे को मिठाई देते हैं।

blocks.heading: Diwali festival
rows: Diwali is the festival of lights, and every lane looks bright with lamps and rangoli.
दीवाली रोशनी का त्योहार है, और हर गली दीयों और रंगोली से जगमगा उठती है।
dīvālī rośanī kā tyohār hai, aur har galī diyõ aur raṅgolī se jagmagā uṭhtī hai.
We clean the whole house and decorate the entrance with marigold garlands before Lakshmi puja.
हम पूरा घर साफ़ करते हैं और लक्ष्मी पूजा से पहले द्वार पर गेंदे की माला लगाते हैं।
ham pūrā ghar sāf karte haiṃ aur lakṣmī pūjā se pahle dvār par geṃde kī mālā lagāte haiṃ.
Could you bring two kilos of pure ghee and a packet of dry fruits for the sweets we will cook?
क्या आप दो किलो शुद्ध घी और एक पैकेट मेवे ला सकते हैं, जिससे हम मिठाई बनाएँगे?
kyā āp do kilo śuddh ghī aur ek pekeṭ meve lā sakte haiṃ, jisse ham miṭhāī banāeṅge?
Children burst crackers only under adult supervision, because safety matters more than noise.
बच्चे पटाखे केवल बड़ों की देखरेख में फोड़ते हैं, क्योंकि शोर से ज़्यादा सुरक्षा ज़रूरी है।
bacce paṭākhe keval baṛõ kī dekherekh mẽ phoṛte haiṃ, kyõki śor se zyādā surakṣā zarūrī hai.
On Diwali evening we wear new clothes, exchange gifts, and wish everyone prosperity and peace.
दीवाली की शाम हम नए कपड़े पहनकर उपहार बाँटते हैं और सबको समृद्धि व शांति की शुभकामनाएँ देते हैं।
dīvālī kī śām ham nae kapṛe pahan kar upahār bā̃ṭte haiṃ aur sabko samṛddhi va śānti kī śubhkāmnāeṃ dete haiṃ.
If guests arrive unexpectedly, we still welcome them warmly and offer tea with homemade snacks.
अगर मेहमान अचानक आ जाएँ तो भी हम उनका गर्मजोशी से स्वागत करते हैं और चाय व घर की चटपटी चीज़ें परोसते हैं।
agar mehmān acānak ā jāeṃ to bhī ham unkā garmajośī se svāgat karte haiṃ aur cāy va ghar kī caṭpaṭī cīzeṃ paroste haiṃ.
The market is crowded, yet shopkeepers patiently explain prices and even pack gifts neatly.
बाज़ार भीड़ से भरा होता है, फिर भी दुकानदार धैर्य से दाम समझाते हैं और उपहार भी सलीके से पैक कर देते हैं।
bāzār bhīṛ se bharā hotā hai, phir bhī dukāndār dhairya se dām samjhāte haiṃ aur upahār bhī salīke se pāk kar dete haiṃ.
After dinner we sit on the terrace, count stars, and remember how Diwali felt in childhood.
रात के खाने के बाद हम छत पर बैठकर तारे गिनते हैं और याद करते हैं कि बचपन में दीवाली कैसी लगती थी।
rāt ke khāne ke bād ham chat par baiṭh kar tāre ginte haiṃ aur yād karte haiṃ ki bacpan mẽ dīvālī kaisī lagtī thī.
My grandmother says that kindness during festivals matters more than how expensive your clothes are.
मेरी दादी कहती हैं कि त्योहारों पर दयालुता कपड़ों की कीमत से ज़्यादा मायने रखती है।
merī dādī kahtī haiṃ ki tyohārõ par dayālutā kapṛõ kī kīmat se zyādā māyne rakhtī hai.
We donate old blankets and food packets to the shelter nearby so that joy is shared beyond our family.
हम पुराने कंबल और खाने के पैकेट पास के आश्रय में दान करते हैं ताकि खुशी हमारे परिवार से आगे बाँटी जाए।
ham purāne kambal aur khāne ke pekeṭ pās ke āśray mẽ dān karte haiṃ tāki khushī hamāre parivār se āge bā̃ṭī jā.e.
When the electricity flickers during the evening, we laugh and light extra diyas without complaining.
शाम को बिजली कभी कभी काँप उठती है, तब हम बिना शिकायत के हँसकर और दीये जला देते हैं।
śām ko bijlī kabhī kabhī kā̃p uṭhtī hai, tab ham binā śikāyat ke hãs kar aur dīye jalā dete haiṃ.
Before sleeping, I message cousins who live abroad and send them photos of our decorated courtyard.
सोने से पहले मैं विदेश में रहने वाले कज़िन्स को संदेश भेजकर आँगन की सजी फोटो भेजता हूँ।
sone se pahle main videś mẽ rahne vāle kazins ko sandeś bhej kar ā̃gan kī sajī phoṭo bhejtā hū̃.

title: Simple Sentences in Hindi – Introduction/Salutation
url: Simple Sentences in Hindi – Introduction/Salutation
content: Introductions and polite salutations for strangers, colleagues, and elders.
intro: Use आप with people you do not know well; तुम only with close peers if appropriate.

blocks.heading: Register
blocks.content: नमस्ते works any time; सुप्रभात is morning-only.

blocks.heading: Introduction / salutation
rows: Good morning, I am pleased to meet you; my name is Rohan and I recently moved to this neighbourhood.
सुप्रभात, आपसे मिलकर खुशी हुई; मेरा नाम रोहन है और मैं हाल ही में इस मोहल्ले में आया हूँ।
suprabhāt, āpse milkar khushī huī; merā nām rohan hai aur main hāl hī mẽ is mohalle mẽ āyā hū̃.
Namaste, I work as a teacher, and I would love to know what you do for a living.
नमस्ते, मैं शिक्षक के रूप में काम करता हूँ, और जानना चाहूँगा कि आप किस क्षेत्र में कार्य करते हैं।
namaste, main śikṣak ke rūp mẽ kām kartā hū̃, aur jānnā cāhū̃gā ki āp kis kṣetra mẽ kāry karte haiṃ.
Please sit down; would you like chilled water or masala tea before we begin the conversation?
कृपया बैठिए; क्या आप बात शुरू करने से पहले ठंडा पानी या मसाला चाय लेंगे?
kripayā baithie; kyā āp bāt śurū karne se pahle thaṃḍā pānī yā masālā cāy leṅge?
I apologize for arriving ten minutes late because the metro was unusually crowded today.
मैं दस मिनट देर से आने के लिए क्षमा चाहता हूँ क्योंकि आज मेट्रो असामान्य रूप से भीड़ थी।
main das minaṭ der se āne ke lie kṣamā cāhtā hū̃ kyõki āj meṭro asāmānya rūp se bhīṛ thī.
It is an honour to meet your parents; they have always supported young artists in our city.
आपके माता-पिता से मिलना सम्मान की बात है; उन्होंने हमारे शहर में हमेशा नए कलाकारों का साथ दिया है।
āp ke mātā-pitā se milnā sammān kī bāt hai; unhoṃne hamāre śahar mẽ hameśā nae kalākārõ kā sāth diyā hai.
Let me introduce my colleague who handles community projects and speaks fluent Hindi and Tamil.
मैं अपने सहकर्मी से मिलवाता हूँ जो सामुदायिक परियोजनाएँ संभालते हैं और हिन्दी-तमिल धाराप्रवाह बोलते हैं।
main apne sahakarmī se milvātā hū̃ jo sāmudāyik pariyojnāeṃ saṃbhālte haiṃ aur hindī-tamil dhārāpravāh bolte haiṃ.
When elders enter the room, we stand up briefly to show respect even if the meeting is informal.
जब बुज़ुर्ग कमरे में आते हैं तो हम थोड़ी देर के लिए खड़े हो जाते हैं, भले ही मीटिंग अनौपचारिक हो।
jab buzurg kamre mẽ āte haiṃ to ham thoṛī der ke lie khaṛe ho jāte haiṃ, bhale hī mīṭiṅ anopacārik ho.
If you ever need help with paperwork, call me directly instead of hesitating on the street.
अगर कागज़ी कार्रवाई में कभी मदद चाहिए तो सड़क पर झिझकने के बजाय सीधे मुझे फोन कर लीजिए।
agar kāgazī kārravāī mẽ kabhī madad cāhie to saṛak par jhijhakne ke bajāy sīdhe mujhe phon kar lijie.
Before leaving, I thanked the host family and promised to bring homemade pickles next Sunday.
जाते समय मैंने मेज़बान परिवार को धन्यवाद दिया और अगले रविवार घर की अचार की बोतल लाने का वादा किया।
jāte samay mainne mezbān parivār ko dhanyavād diyā aur agale ravivār ghar kī acār kī botal lāne kā vādā kiyā.
She smiled and said that warm greetings make even a new city feel a little like home.
वह मुस्कुराई और बोली कि गर्मजोशी भरे अभिवादन से नया शहर भी थोड़ा घर जैसा लगने लगता है।
vah muskurāī aur bolī ki garmajośī bhare abhivādan se nayā śahar bhī thoṛā ghar jaise lagne lagtā hai.
We exchanged business cards and agreed to continue the discussion over lunch later this week.
हमने विज़िटिंग कार्ड साझा किए और इस हफ़्ते बाद दोपहर के खाने पर चर्चा जारी रखने पर सहमति जताई।
hamne viziṭiṅg kārd sājhā kie aur is hafte bād dopahar ke khāne par carcā jārī rakhne par sahamti jatāī.
Remember to bow slightly when you greet the priest at the temple; it signals humility and respect.
मंदिर में पुजारी जी को नमस्ते करते समय हल्का झुकना याद रखिए; यह विनम्रता और सम्मान दिखाता है।
mandir mẽ pujārī jī ko namaste karte samay halkā jhuknā yād rakhie; yah vinamratā aur sammān dikhātā hai.

title: Simple Hindi Sentences – Time Related
url: Simple Hindi Sentences – Time Related
content: Time expressions: clock time, routines, deadlines, and polite scheduling.
intro: Pair बजे with numbers; use कल carefully for yesterday vs tomorrow by context.

blocks.heading: Clock
blocks.content: सात बज रहे हैं means “it is seven” in progress; सात बजे is “at seven”.

blocks.heading: Time-related sentences
rows: The workshop begins sharply at nine thirty, so please reach the gate ten minutes early.
कार्यशाला साढ़े नौ बजे ठीक शुरू होती है, इसलिए कृपया दस मिनट पहले गेट पर पहुँच जाइए।
kāryaśālā sāṛhe nau baje ṭhīk śurū hotī hai, islie kripayā das minaṭ pahle geṭ par pahuṃc jāie.
Yesterday I missed the train because I misunderstood whether seven meant morning or evening.
कल मेरी ट्रेन छूट गई क्योंकि मैंने समझा ही नहीं कि सात का मतलब सुबह है या शाम।
kal merī ṭren chūṭ gaī kyõki mainne samjhā hī nahī̃ ki sāt kā matlab subah hai yā śām.
Could we postpone the call to tomorrow afternoon when my internet connection is usually stable?
क्या हम कॉल कल दोपहर के लिए टाल सकते हैं जब मेरा इंटरनेट आमतौर पर स्थिर रहता है?
kyā ham kŏl kal dopahar ke lie ṭāl sakte haiṃ jab merā intarnet āmtaur par sthir rehtā hai?
Right now I am stuck in traffic, but I promise I will reach before the bank closes at four.
अभी मैं ट्रैफ़िक में फँसा हूँ, पर वादा करता हूँ कि बैंक चार बजे बंद होने से पहले पहुँच जाऊँगा।
abhī main ṭraifik mẽ phãsā hū̃, par vādā kartā hū̃ ki baiṅk cār baje band hone se pahle pahuṃc jāū̃gā.
She wakes up before sunrise so that she can finish yoga and still cook breakfast for the children.
वह सूरज निकलने से पहले उठ जाती है ताकि योग कर सके और फिर भी बच्चों के लिए नाश्ता बना सके।
vah sūraj nikalne se pahle uṭh jātī hai tāki yog kar sake aur phir bhī baccõ ke lie nāśtā banā sake.
We have been waiting since three o’clock, yet the technician keeps saying he will arrive in five minutes.
हम तीन बजे से इंतज़ार कर रहे हैं, फिर भी टेक्नीशियन बार-बार कहता है कि वह पाँच मिनट में आएगा।
ham tīn baje se intazār kar rahe haiṃ, phir bhī ṭeknīśiyan bār-bār kahtā hai ki vah pā̃c minaṭ mẽ āegā.
During winter the sun sets early, so we schedule outdoor games only until five in the evening.
सर्दियों में सूरज जल्दी डूब जाता है, इसलिए हम बाहर के खेल केवल शाम पाँच बजे तक रखते हैं।
sardiyõ mẽ sūraj jaldī ḍūb jātā hai, islie ham bāhar ke khel keval śām pā̃c baje tak rakhte haiṃ.
By the time I finish this report, midnight will have passed and the streets will be completely quiet.
जब तक मैं यह रिपोर्ट खत्म करूँ, आधी रात गुज़र चुकी होगी और गलियाँ पूरी तरह शांत होंगी।
jab tak main yah ripoṭ khatm karū̃, ādhī rāt guzar cukī hogī aur galiyā̃ pūrī tarah śānt hoṅgī.
Please remind me two hours before the flight because I easily lose track of time while reading.
कृपया उड़ान से दो घंटे पहले याद दिलाइएगा क्योंकि पढ़ते समय मुझे समय का पता ही नहीं चलता।
kripayā uṛān se do ghaṃṭe pahle yād dilāiega kyõki paṛhte samay mujhe samay kā patā hī nahī̃ caltā.
Every Friday evening our book club meets for three hours and discusses one novel in depth.
हर शुक्रवार शाम हमारा बुक क्लब तीन घंटे मिलता है और एक उपन्यास पर गहराई से चर्चा करता है।
har śukravār śām hamārā buk klub tīn ghaṃṭe miltā hai aur ek upanyās par gahrāī se carcā kartā hai.
The museum opens at ten, closes at six, and stays shut on Mondays for maintenance work.
संग्रहालय दस बजे खुलता है, छह बजे बंद होता है, और रखरखाव के लिए सोमवार को बंद रहता है।
saṅgrahālay das baje khultā hai, chah baje band hotā hai, aur rakh-rakhāv ke lie somvār ko band rehtā hai.
He promised to text me the moment the parcel leaves the warehouse so that I can stay informed.
उसने वादा किया कि जैसे ही पार्सल गोदाम से निकलेगा वह मुझे संदेश भेज देगा ताकि मुझे पता रहे।
usne vādā kiyā ki jaise hī pārsal godām se niklegā vah mujhe sandeś bhej degā tāki mujhe patā rahe.

title: Simple Hindi Sentences – Where/Place Related
url: Simple Hindi Sentences – Where/Place Related
content: Places and directions: landmarks, public transport, and clarifying “which side”.
intro: Use इधर/उधर and सामने for quick pointing; repeat the landmark if the listener looks confused.

blocks.heading: Directions
blocks.content: पहले सीधे जाइए, फिर बाएँ मुड़िए — first go straight, then turn left.

blocks.heading: Where / place
rows: Excuse me, could you tell me whether the new library is behind the old post office or beside it?
माफ़ कीजिए, क्या आप बता सकते हैं कि नई लाइब्रेरी पुराने डाकखाने के पीछे है या उसके बगल में?
māf kījie, kyā āp batā sakte haiṃ ki naī lāibrerī purāne ḍākkhāne ke pīche hai yā us ke bagal mẽ?
I am looking for a quiet café where I can sit for two hours and finish this translation.
मैं ऐसा शांत कैफ़े ढूँढ रहा हूँ जहाँ दो घंटे बैठकर यह अनुवाद खत्म कर सकूँ।
main aisā śānt kaife ḍhū̃ṛ rahā hū̃ jahā̃ do ghaṃṭe baiṭh kar yah anuvād khatm kar sakū̃.
The pharmacy on the corner stays open until midnight, which is helpful when children fall ill suddenly.
कोने वाली दवा की दुकान आधी रात तक खुली रहती है, जो जब बच्चे अचानक बीमार पड़ें तो बहुत काम आती है।
kone vālī davā kī dukān ādhī rāt tak khulī rehtī hai, jo jab bacce acānak bīmār paṛeṃ to bahut kām ātī hai.
We parked near the river because the underground lot was full of wedding guests’ cars.
हमने नदी के पास गाड़ी खड़ी की क्योंकि अंडरग्राउंड पार्किंग शादी के मेहमानों की कारों से भरी थी।
hamne nadī ke pās gāṛī khaṛī kī kyõki aṇḍargrauṇḍ pārkiṅg śādī ke mehmānõ kī kārõ se bharī thī.
If you cross the footbridge, you will reach the platform without dragging luggage through mud.
अगर आप फुटओवर पार कर लें तो कीचड़ में सामान घसीटे बिना ही प्लेटफ़ॉर्म तक पहुँच जाएँगे।
agar āp phuṭovar pār kar leṃ to kīcaṛ mẽ sāmān ghasīṭe binā hī pleṭphŏrm tak pahuṃc jāeṃge.
There is a small park where senior citizens walk every morning and share news from newspapers.
एक छोटा पार्क है जहाँ बुज़ुर्ग हर सुबह टहलते हैं और अख़बारों की ख़बरें साझा करते हैं।
ek choṭā pārk hai jahā̃ buzurg har subah ṭahalte haiṃ aur akhbārõ kī khabreṃ sājhā karte haiṃ.
My cousin’s new apartment faces the hills, so the balcony stays cool even in May.
मेरे कज़िन का नया फ़्लैट पहाड़ों की ओर है, इसलिए मई में भी बालकनी ठंडी रहती है।
mere kazin kā nayā flaiṭ pahāḍõ kī or hai, islie maī mẽ bhī bālkonī thaṃḍī rehtī hai.
The lane is narrow, yet delivery vans squeeze through because residents need groceries daily.
गली तंग है, फिर भी डिलीवरी वैन निकल जाती हैं क्योंकि रहवासियों को रोज़ाना किराना चाहिए।
galī taṅg hai, phir bhī ḍilīvarī vain nikal jātī haiṃ kyõki rahvāsiyõ ko rozānā kirānā cāhie.

usne pūchā ki hŏsṭal viśvavidyālay se paidal kī dūrī par hai yā ôṭo lenī cāhie.
We finally found the community hall after circling the temple three times and asking a tea seller.
हमें समुदायिक हॉल तब मिला जब हम तीन बार मंदिर का चक्कर लगा चुके थे और एक चायवाले से पूछ लिया।
hameṃ samudāyik hŏl tab milā jab ham tīn bār mandir kā cakkar lagā cuke the aur ek cāyvāle se pūch liyā.
The signboard is faded, but if you look carefully you will still read “City Hospital, Emergency Gate”.
साइनबोर्ड फीका पड़ गया है, पर ध्यान से देखेंगे तो अभी भी “सिटी हॉस्पिटल, इमरजेंसी गेट” पढ़ सकते हैं।
sāinbŏrḍ phīkā paṛ gayā hai, par dhyān se dekheṅge to abhī bhī “siṭī hŏspiṭal, imarjeṃsī geṭ” paṛh sakte haiṃ.

title: Simple Hindi Sentences – In Hotel
url: Simple Hindi Sentences – In Hotel
content: Hotel stay: check-in, room preferences, housekeeping, and checkout politely.
intro: Ask for a printed bill; confirm whether breakfast is included in the tariff.

blocks.heading: Front desk
blocks.content: क्या मेरा कमरा अब तैयार है? — Is my room ready yet?

blocks.heading: In a hotel
rows: Good evening, I have a reservation under the name Meera Sharma for two nights starting today.
शुभ संध्या, मेरा आज से दो रातों का आरक्षण मीरा शर्मा के नाम पर है।
śubh sandhyā, merā āj se do rātõ kā ārakṣaṇ mīrā śarmā ke nām par hai.
Could we have two key cards, please, because my colleague and I will return at different times?
क्या हमें दो की-कार्ड मिल सकते हैं क्योंकि मैं और मेरा सहकर्मी अलग-अलग समय लौटेंगे?
kyā hameṃ do kī-kārḍ mil sakte haiṃ kyõki main aur merā sahakarmī alag-alag samay lauṭeṅge?
The room on the higher floor is fine, but please make sure the window closes tightly against traffic noise.
ऊँची मंज़िल का कमरा ठीक है, पर कृपया यह सुनिश्चित करें कि खिड़की शोर के लिए कसकर बंद हो।
ū̃cī manzil kā kamrā ṭhīk hai, par kripayā yah suniścit kareṃ ki khiṛkī śor ke lie kas kar band ho.
